---
title: "ML models exercise"
---

First to install packages
```{r}
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidymodels)
library(plyr)
library(corrplot)
```

Then to load data and set seed
```{r}
data_location <- here::here("ml-models-exercise", "clean.rds")
df <- readRDS(data_location)
set.seed(1234)
```

Change race 7 and 88 to category 3
```{r}
df$RACE <- revalue(df$RACE, c("7" = "3", "88" = "3"))
```

Make a pairwise correlation plot for continuous variables
```{r}
cor_df <- df %>% select(-DOSE, -SEX, -RACE)
cor_plot1 <- corrplot::corrplot(cor(cor_df, use = "complete.obs"), method = "number", type = "upper")
cor_plot1
```
Nothing seems super correlated!

Compute BMI
I am going to assume height is in meters, and weight in kg
Plugging random values into a calculator and converting into imperial gives me weights and heights that make sense
The formula I found is weight(kg)/height(m)^2
```{r}
df1 <- df %>% mutate(BMI = WT/(HT*HT))
```
